acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] beads-git crate exists and compiles independently
- [ ] beads-daemon-core owns WAL impl with feature-gated sqlite
- [ ] beads-daemon owns daemon runtime (server, executor, mutation engine, query, store, admin, repl orchestration)
- [ ] beads-rs is a thin facade (<6k lines source)
- [ ] Zero beads_rs::daemon::* imports outside beads-rs itself
- [ ] DAG policy covers all crates including dev-deps
- [ ] cargo check/test/clippy/dylint all pass
description:
**Problem**
beads-rs has a 38k-line daemon monolith and a 10k-line git module inside a single crate. Despite good internal boundaries (Phase II), crate-level enforcement is incomplete — agents can still couple across subsystem boundaries because \`pub mod daemon\` exposes everything. The codebase needs compiler-enforced isolation (crate boundaries as locked rooms) for local reasoning, agent-proofing, and eventual cross-language consumption.

**Design**
Decompose beads-rs into well-bounded crates. One new crate (\`beads-git\`), expand two existing ones (\`beads-daemon-core\` absorbs WAL impl, \`beads-daemon\` absorbs daemon runtime). beads-rs shrinks to a ~5k-line thin facade.

End state:
- beads-core (20k) — types, CRDTs, events (unchanged)
- beads-api (2k) — IPC schemas (unchanged)
- beads-surface (4k) — IPC client, ops/query (unchanged)
- beads-cli (9k) — CLI parse/dispatch (unchanged)
- beads-git (10k) — NEW: git sync + checkpoint
- beads-daemon-core (14k) — EXPAND: WAL impl + repl protocol + contracts
- beads-daemon (23k) — EXPAND: full daemon runtime
- beads-rs (5k) — SHRINK: binary entrypoint + config + compat

Critical path:
- Policy cleanup (unblocks enforcement)
- Injection types + break reverse dep (unblocks extraction, parallelizable)
- Extract beads-git (parallelizable with WAL move)
- Absorb WAL into daemon-core (parallelizable with git extraction)
- Move daemon runtime into beads-daemon (blocked by above)
- Add testkit + migrate fixtures (blocked by daemon move)
- Narrow beads-rs exports (final cleanup)

**Acceptance**
- [ ] \`beads-git\` crate exists with git sync + checkpoint, zero daemon imports
- [ ] \`beads-daemon-core\` owns full WAL implementation (feature-gated)
- [ ] \`beads-daemon\` owns full daemon runtime with \`pub(crate)\` internals
- [ ] \`beads-rs\` is <6k lines, no \`pub mod daemon\`
- [ ] All tests pass, all boundary lints pass
- [ ] DAG policy updated and enforced including dev-deps
- [ ] An agent dropped into any single crate can reason about it locally
title: Phase III: crate decomposition for local reasoning + agent-proofing

acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] It is impossible to represent a sealed segment without `final_len` (compile‑time enforced).
- [ ] DB load rejects invalid rows before creating `SegmentRow`.
- [ ] Replay/fsck no longer needs to check for sealed+None at runtime.
- [ ] Tests cover open vs sealed row serialization and invalid DB row rejection.
description:
**Problem**
`SegmentRow` in the WAL index uses `sealed: bool` + `final_len: Option<u64>`. This allows invalid states like `sealed=true` with `final_len=None`, which are only caught at replay/fsck time.

Key refs:
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/index.rs:198` (SegmentRow)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/replay.rs:264` (runtime error when sealed missing final_len)

**Impact**
Index invariants are not enforced at compile time; sealed segment metadata can be corrupt without immediate detection.
design:
**Design (opinionated)**
Make sealed‑segment invariants structural.

Option A (enum row):
```rust
enum SegmentRow {
  Open { /* fields */, last_indexed_offset: u64 },
  Sealed { /* fields */, final_len: u64 },
}
```
- No `Option` for final_len; sealed implies `final_len` exists.
- Encode/decode to DB enforces the invariant when loading rows.

Option B (struct + NonZero marker):
- `final_len: FinalLen` where `FinalLen` is only constructible when sealed, but enum is cleaner.

Update replay/index logic to accept typed rows so invalid state cannot compile.
title: Encode WAL segment sealed/final_len invariants

description:
Problem: admin-metrics snapshot is cumulative across daemon lifetime, so request histogram maxima include startup/warm-up operations and can mislead steady-state comparisons (e.g., ready max).

Design: add a daemon metrics reset/snapshot-since capability (or benchmark-side daemon restart before measured phase) so steady-state histograms can be isolated without manual parsing.

Acceptance:
- [ ] Benchmark can report steady-state admin histograms without startup contamination
- [ ] docs/PERF_HOTPATHS.md documents expected behavior and workflow
- [ ] regression test or scripted check validates boundary behavior
title: Admin metrics include startup warm-up outliers in hotpath runs
